name: 01 Systemd
title: Systemd Deep Dive: The System Architect
description: The definitive and exhaustive guide on PID 1. From Unit anatomy and dependency graphs to Cgroups, boot optimization, and creating production daemons.
path: Arborito Library (EN) / Engineering & Technology / Information Technology: Operating Systems / Linux Ecosystem / Advanced Linux SysAdmin / Module 1: Advanced System Control / 01 Systemd

name: 02 Kernel Compilation
title: The Heart of the Beast: Kernel and Modules
description: Learn to manipulate the Kernel in real time with sysctl, manage modules with modprobe, understand Kernel Tainting, and the definitive guide to compiling your own Linux Kernel from scratch.
path: Arborito Library (EN) / Engineering & Technology / Information Technology: Operating Systems / Linux Ecosystem / Advanced Linux SysAdmin / Module 1: Advanced System Control / 02 Kernel Compilation

name: 03 Disks LVM RAID
title: Advanced Storage: LVM and RAID
description: The master guide (+1000 lines) to mastering storage in Linux. Leave static partitions behind and learn to manage space like a fluid. Software RAID (mdadm), Logical Volumes (LVM), Snapshots, Thin Provisioning, and disaster recovery.
path: Arborito Library (EN) / Engineering & Technology / Information Technology: Operating Systems / Linux Ecosystem / Advanced Linux SysAdmin / Module 1: Advanced System Control / 03 Disks LVM RAID

name: 04 Advanced Filesystems
title: Next Generation Filesystems: ZFS and Btrfs
description: The master guide (+1000 lines). Discover the power of Copy-on-Write (CoW), data self-healing, atomic snapshots, transparent compression, and integrated volume management.
path: Arborito Library (EN) / Engineering & Technology / Information Technology: Operating Systems / Linux Ecosystem / Advanced Linux SysAdmin / Module 1: Advanced System Control / 04 Advanced Filesystems

name: 05 Task Scheduling
title: System Watchmaker: Cron and Systemd Timers
description: Advanced temporal automation. From crontab syntax to Systemd Timers precision, managing asynchronous tasks with 'at' and 'systemd-run'.
path: Arborito Library (EN) / Engineering & Technology / Information Technology: Operating Systems / Linux Ecosystem / Advanced Linux SysAdmin / Module 1: Advanced System Control / 05 Task Scheduling
